title: cassine ehrenbergii
intro: Cassine ehrenbergii, also known as Cassine or Wild Privet, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Cassine ehrenbergii is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating digestive problems. Cassine ehrenbergii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves are often used in herbal remedies, while its roots are used to treat skin conditions and wounds.
